rulings.Case Title: LAUREN HAULE VS. NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Production Of Documents Pursuant To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 29, 2014, Line 9: PLAINTIFF LAUREN HAULE'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pursuant To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two Granted, as to Request nos. 53 - 58, 63. Denied as to Request nos. 59 - 62. Defendant has placed Plaintiff's non-work related emails at issue in this case. Plaintiff is entitled to conduct limited discovery into whether certain other employees engaged in similar conduct. Within 20 days, Defendant New Deal Design, LLC ("Defendant") shall serve further responses and produce responsive documents. For Request nos. 53 - 58, 63, except for President and founder Gadi Ami, the non-work related activities of other employees is discoverable. The discovery sought is limited to emails sent by employees with certain phrases or links and does not implicate the privacy rights of the third party employees. Defendant has not demonstrated that this discovery is an unduly burdensome. The emails that are the subject of the discovery requests are limited to specific subject lines. For Request nos. 59 - 60, Defendant has responded to these requests, and they are no longer at issue. For Request no. 61 - 62, as the President and founder, Mr. Ami is not a similarly situated comparator to Plaintiff. The probative value is insufficient to warrant discovery. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE S. BLANZA VS. NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 29, 2014, Line 13: DEFENDANTS ALLIANT SERVICES HOUSTON, INC.'S and ALLIANT INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled and sustained as follows: The Court overrules the demurrer to causes of action 3-5 because plaintiff's fraud allegations against defendants are pleaded with sufficient particularity. Plaintiff alleges that defendants' advertisements over the course of a decade misled him. (Pars. 8 and 13 of the Complaint). The Court overrules the demurrer to cause of action 6. Plaintiff alleges that defendants converted his premiums. A simple accounting can determine the amount of the premiums and it is a specific identifiable sum that will support a conversion cause of action. The Court overrules the demurrer to cause of action 7. Plaintiff alleges that defendants took part in mis-administering plaintiff's claim at paragraph 13. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 8 without leave to amend. The complaint alleges that the defendants were joint tortfeasors and the Prentice rule does not apply. See Vacco Industries, Inc. v. Van Den Berg (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 34, 57. Plaintiff's allegation at paragraph 5 that defendants were acting as individuals does not save the claim; the parties were still allegedly joint tortfeasors. The Court rejects defendants' arguments based on agency because it is premature to determine exactly what role defendants were playing vis a vis the other defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE S. BLANZA VS. NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 29, 2014, Line 13: DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS THE COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG, PA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
